title: lappula texana var. heterosperma
intro: Lappula texana var. heterosperma, also known as Texas bluebells or prickly globetrotter, is a flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lappula texana var. heterosperma include treating various ailments such as fever, cough, sore throat, and digestive issues, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, extracting the active compounds with a solvent, or mixing the plant material with a carrier oil to create a topical remedy.
